ogTitle: Hacks Ferry vs Halekulani: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hacks Ferry and Halekulani.
heading: Hacks Ferry vs Halekulani — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hacks Ferry, New South Wales and Halekulani, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hacks Ferry has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Halekulani (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hacks Ferry or Halekulani safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hacks Ferry has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Halekulani scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hacks Ferry scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Halekulani scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hacks Ferry or Halekulani?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hacks Ferry has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
